subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Help the Story Solver Crew match math equations to everyday problems.
lesson.mini_lesson: Word problems tell a short story with known numbers and an unknown. Decide whether to add, subtract, compare, or do two steps.
lesson.objective: Solve one-step and two-step addition and subtraction word problems within 100.
lesson.concept_explanation: Use the words and model to decide the operation. Unknowns can be the result, a part, or the starting amount.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may choose an operation from one keyword instead of reading the whole story.
lesson.real_world_connection: Word problems help solve questions about supplies, scores, collections, and lengths.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read, model, write an equation, solve, and check the answer against the story.
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

worked_examples.text: Mia has 24 shells and gets 18 more, so 24 + 18 = 42.
A jar had 65 marbles and 19 rolled out, so 65 - 19 = 46.
For two steps, solve the first action before the second action.

hint_ladder: Read the story and find what is asked.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

misconception_bank.operation_selection_error: Learner may add when the story calls for subtraction, or subtract when the story calls for adding.
misconception_bank.missing_unknown_confusion: Learner may not notice whether the unknown is the start, change, or result.
misconception_bank.compare_problem_confusion: Learner may add both compared amounts instead of finding the difference.
misconception_bank.two_step_tracking_error: Learner may answer after only one step or lose the intermediate result.

game_theme.title: Addition and Subtraction Word Problems
game_theme.mission_intro: Help the Story Solver Crew match math equations to everyday problems.

guided_practice.prompt: Mia has 24 shells. Leo gives her 18 more. How many shells does Mia have now?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

guided_practice.explanation: 24 + 18 = 42. Add to put together or find the missing addend.

question_audio.text: Mia has 24 shells. Leo gives her 18 more. How many shells does Mia have now?
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.prompt: There are 36 red apples and 27 green apples. How many apples are there altogether?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

guided_practice.explanation: 36 + 27 = 63. Add to put together or find the missing addend.

question_audio.text: There are 36 red apples and 27 green apples. How many apples are there altogether?
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.prompt: A class reads 29 books in March and 31 in April. How many books did they read in all?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

guided_practice.explanation: 29 + 31 = 60. Add to put together or find the missing addend.

question_audio.text: A class reads 29 books in March and 31 in April. How many books did they read in all?
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.prompt: There were 45 birds in a tree. 16 more landed. How many birds are in the tree now?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

guided_practice.explanation: 45 + 16 = 61. Add to put together or find the missing addend.

question_audio.text: There were 45 birds in a tree. 16 more landed. How many birds are in the tree now?
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.prompt: Sam packed 38 crayons and 22 markers. How many art tools did Sam pack?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

guided_practice.explanation: 38 + 22 = 60. Add to put together or find the missing addend.

question_audio.text: Sam packed 38 crayons and 22 markers. How many art tools did Sam pack?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: A class collected 24 cans Monday and 31 cans Tuesday. They recycled 18 cans. How many cans are left?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Solve one step, then use that result for the next step: 24 + 31 - 18 = 37.

question_audio.text: A class collected 24 cans Monday and 31 cans Tuesday. They recycled 18 cans. How many cans are left?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Ben had 70 stickers. He gave 25 away and then got 14 more. How many stickers now?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Solve one step, then use that result for the next step: 70 - 25 + 14 = 59.

question_audio.text: Ben had 70 stickers. He gave 25 away and then got 14 more. How many stickers now?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: There are 19 red, 22 blue, and 17 green balloons. How many balloons are there in all?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Solve one step, then use that result for the next step: 19 + 22 + 17 = 58.

question_audio.text: There are 19 red, 22 blue, and 17 green balloons. How many balloons are there in all?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: A bus had 64 riders. 18 got off and 23 got on. How many riders are on the bus?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Solve one step, then use that result for the next step: 64 - 18 + 23 = 69.

question_audio.text: A bus had 64 riders. 18 got off and 23 got on. How many riders are on the bus?
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Lily read 28 pages Saturday and 26 Sunday. Her book has 90 pages. How many pages are left?
hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Solve one step, then use that result for the next step: 90 - (28 + 26) = 36.

question_audio.text: Lily read 28 pages Saturday and 26 Sunday. Her book has 90 pages. How many pages are left?
question_audio.label: Read Question

hints: Read the story and find what is asked.
Choose add, subtract, or compare.
Write an equation and solve the unknown.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

checkpoint.explanation: Compare by subtracting the smaller amount from the larger amount: 46 - 29 = 17.

questions.prompt: Mia has 24 shells. Leo gives her 18 more. How many shells does Mia have now?
hints: Focus: In G2M_WP_001_LVL1_Q1, identify the two known quantities and the unknown result.
Strategy: Represent the story with 24 + 18 = the unknown.
Verify: Subtract one addend from your total; you should recover the other addend.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: 24 + 18 = 42. Why it works: the story joins the two amounts into one total. Check with the inverse operation: 42 - 18 = 24.

question_audio.text: Mia has 24 shells. Leo gives her 18 more. How many shells does Mia have now? Visual context: The bar model shows known quantities 24 and 18, with the result unknown. A question mark marks the quantity to find.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Nora found 17 rocks. Then she found some more. Now she has 49 rocks. How many more rocks did she find?
hints: Focus: In G2M_WP_001_LVL1_Q6, identify the unknown change between the known start and result.
Strategy: Represent the story with 17 + the unknown = 49.
Verify: Subtract one addend from your total; you should recover the other addend.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: 17 + 32 = 32. Why it works: the story joins the two amounts into one total. Check with the inverse operation: 32 - 32 = 17.

question_audio.text: Nora found 17 rocks. Then she found some more. Now she has 49 rocks. How many more rocks did she find? Visual context: The word problem model shows known start and result quantities 17 and 49, with the change unknown. A question mark marks the quantity to find.

questions.prompt: A shelf has some picture books. 25 chapter books are added. Now there are 64 books. How many picture books were on the shelf?
hints: Focus: In G2M_WP_001_LVL1_Q7, identify the unknown starting amount before the change.
Strategy: Use the inverse operation on 25 and 64 to find the start.
Verify: Add your start to 25; the total should be 64.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: 64 - 25 = 39, so the unknown start is 39. Why it works: subtraction undoes the added 25. Check with the inverse operation: 39 + 25 = 64.

question_audio.text: A shelf has some picture books. 25 chapter books are added. Now there are 64 books. How many picture books were on the shelf? Visual context: The bar model shows an unknown starting amount and known quantities 25 and 64. A question mark marks the quantity to find.
question_audio.label: Read Question

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: 42 + 18 = 60, so the starting amount was 60. Why it works: adding back what was removed rebuilds the start. Check with the inverse operation: 60 - 18 = 42.

questions.prompt: Ravi has 46 stamps. Kim has 29 stamps. How many more stamps does Ravi have than Kim?

Strategy: Subtract 29 from 46 to measure the gap.
Verify: Add the difference to 29; the sum should be 46.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: 46 - 29 = 17. Why it works: subtraction measures the gap between the larger and smaller amounts. Check with inverse addition: 29 + 17 = 46.

level_banks.label: Level 4: Two-Step Problems
level_banks.focus: Two-step addition and subtraction word problems within 100

questions.prompt: A class collected 24 cans Monday and 31 cans Tuesday. They recycled 18 cans. How many cans are left?
hints: Focus: In G2M_WP_001_LVL4_Q1, identify the order of the two changes in the story.
Strategy: Follow the story in order using 24 + 31 - 18 = the unknown.
Verify: Undo the final step, then check the first step against 24 + 31 - 18 = 37.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: First, 24 + 31 = 55. Then, 55 - 18 = 37. Why it works: solving each action in story order tracks the changing amount. Check: undo the last step to return to 55, then undo the first to return to 24.

question_audio.text: A class collected 24 cans Monday and 31 cans Tuesday. They recycled 18 cans. How many cans are left? Visual context: The word problem model shows a two-step relationship using 24, 31, 18. A question mark marks the quantity to find.
question_audio.label: Read Question

level_banks.label: Mixed
level_banks.focus: Mixed addition, subtraction, compare, and two-step word problems

questions.prompt: A nature club placed 26 seed packets in one bin and 17 in another. How many seed packets are in the two bins altogether?
hints: Focus: In G2M_WP_001_MIXED_Q1, identify the two known quantities and the unknown result.
Strategy: Represent the story with 26 + 17 = the unknown.
Verify: Subtract one addend from your total; you should recover the other addend.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: 26 + 17 = 43. Why it works: the story joins the two amounts into one total. Check with the inverse operation: 43 - 17 = 26.

question_audio.text: A nature club placed 26 seed packets in one bin and 17 in another. How many seed packets are in the two bins altogether? Visual context: The equation builder shows known quantities 26 and 17, with the result unknown. A question mark marks the quantity to find.

hints: Focus: In G2M_WP_001_MIXED_Q2, identify the unknown starting amount before the change.
Strategy: Use the inverse operation on 23 and 71 to find the start.
Verify: Add your start to 23; the total should be 71.

questions.explanation: 71 - 23 = 48, so the unknown start is 48. Why it works: subtraction undoes the added 23. Check with the inverse operation: 48 + 23 = 71.

questions.prompt: At field day, 21 students chose hoops and 33 chose jump ropes. Then 16 students returned equipment. How many students still had equipment?
hints: Focus: In G2M_WP_001_MIXED_Q8, identify the order of the two changes in the story.
Strategy: Follow the story in order using 21 + 33 - 16 = the unknown.
Verify: Undo the final step, then check the first step against 21 + 33 - 16 = 38.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: First, 21 + 33 = 54. Then, 54 - 16 = 38. Why it works: solving each action in story order tracks the changing amount. Check: undo the last step to return to 54, then undo the first to return to 21.

question_audio.text: At field day, 21 students chose hoops and 33 chose jump ropes. Then 16 students returned equipment. How many students still had equipment? Visual context: The equation builder shows a two-step relationship using 21, 33, 16. A question mark marks the quantity to find.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: The equation builder shows a two-step relationship using 21, 33, 16. A question mark marks the quantity to find.
questions.accessible_description: The equation builder shows a two-step relationship using 21, 33, 16. A question mark marks the quantity to find.

questions.prompt: A bakery displayed 88 rolls. It sold 34 in the morning and added 12 fresh rolls. How many rolls were then displayed?
hints: Focus: In G2M_WP_001_MIXED_Q9, identify the order of the two changes in the story.
Strategy: Follow the story in order using 88 - 34 + 12 = the unknown.
Verify: Undo the final step, then check the first step against 88 - 34 + 12 = 66.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: First, 88 - 34 = 54. Then, 54 + 12 = 66. Why it works: solving each action in story order tracks the changing amount. Check: undo the last step to return to 54, then undo the first to return to 88.

question_audio.text: A bakery displayed 88 rolls. It sold 34 in the morning and added 12 fresh rolls. How many rolls were then displayed? Visual context: The bar model shows a two-step relationship using 88, 34, 12. A question mark marks the quantity to find.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: The bar model shows a two-step relationship using 88, 34, 12. A question mark marks the quantity to find.
questions.accessible_description: The bar model shows a two-step relationship using 88, 34, 12. A question mark marks the quantity to find.

questions.prompt: A museum had 73 visitors. Then 18 left, and later 29 more arrived. How many visitors were at the museum then?
hints: Focus: In G2M_WP_001_MIXED_Q10, identify the order of the two changes in the story.
Strategy: Follow the story in order using 73 - 18 + 29 = the unknown.
Verify: Undo the final step, then check the first step against 73 - 18 + 29 = 84.

feedback.incorrect: Use the visual model and try again.

questions.explanation: First, 73 - 18 = 55. Then, 55 + 29 = 84. Why it works: solving each action in story order tracks the changing amount. Check: undo the last step to return to 55, then undo the first to return to 73.

question_audio.text: A museum had 73 visitors. Then 18 left, and later 29 more arrived. How many visitors were at the museum then? Visual context: The word problem model shows a two-step relationship using 73, 18, 29. A question mark marks the quantity to find.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: The word problem model shows a two-step relationship using 73, 18, 29. A question mark marks the quantity to find.
questions.accessible_description: The word problem model shows a two-step relationship using 73, 18, 29. A question mark marks the quantity to find.

story.text: Listen to this page. You are starting a math mission about Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Look at the story and think about what the characters need to solve. Math missions are easier when you name the numbers and the model first. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson teaches Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Look at the word problem model and connect each part to the math words. Use the vocabulary on the page to explain your thinking. Remember to check place value, units, or symbols before you answer. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This page lets you watch a worked example for Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Follow the model one step at a time. Say what each number means before you look at the final answer. If a symbol appears, read it in words like plus, minus, equals, greater than, or less than. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo helps you try Addition and Subtraction Word Problems with support. Build or study the model first, then connect it to the question. A good strategy is to explain the answer in math words before choosing. Press Next when you are ready for practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is practice. Use the visual model to help you think about Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This is a challenge. Use the visual model to help you think about Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This is your checkpoint. Use the visual model to help you think about Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your mission work on Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Look at your stars and remember the strategy you used. Mistakes help show what to practice next. Save your growth data to continue.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your growth for Addition and Subtraction Word Problems. Look at attempts, correct answers, accuracy, and hints used. Use the recommendation to decide what to practice next. You can continue to Skill Practice or replay the mission.
profile.label: Read This Page
